articles.title: Makerfield by-election: Crunching the numbers - why Burnham's win is so significant
articles.description: An unprecedented contest ended in a seismic result, as Andy Burnham was announced as the new Labour MP for Makerfield with 55% of the vote, more than 20 points ahead of his defeated Reform opponent Robert Kenyon. A seat whose wards swung hard to Reform last month in local elections has now swung back just as hard to Labour. With Labour trailing Reform by double digits in national polls, the Greater Manchester Mayor achieved a bigger win over Reform, on a higher turnout, than his predecessor Josh Simons managed in 2024.
